title: Quiz: normalization vs duplicating data
prompt_md: What is the core tradeoff between normalizing data (each fact stored once) and denormalizing it (copying/duplicating data across tables)?
options: Denormalization uses less storage than normalization in every case
Normalization makes writes impossible, so you must denormalize to insert rows
Normalization is always better; duplicating data is never acceptable
Normalization reduces duplication and keeps data consistent, but may need joins; denormalization speeds up reads at the cost of duplicated, harder-to-keep-in-sync data
explanation_md: Normalization stores each fact in one place — less duplication, easier consistency — but answering questions may require joining tables. Denormalization copies or pre-computes data to avoid joins and speed up reads, accepting the risk that the duplicated copies drift out of sync. It's a deliberate tradeoff, not an absolute rule; denormalizing typically *increases* storage, and normalized schemas write just fine.
